title: Gate evidence bundle (v0.5)
description: One readable JSON document binding per-stage in-toto-shaped Statement entries to content-addressed subjects at the bundle top level: the PR head commit plus one sha256 digest per ingested input file, optionally wrapped in a self-signed DSSE envelope. Contract v0.5 — v0 (LOCKED — resolution of TzolkinB/skills#102) plus an additive `EMPTY` execution result (TzolkinB/skills#111, v0.1, ADR-0031) plus the internal witness:// -> gate:// identifier rename (TzolkinB/skills#113, v0.2, ADR-0033) plus the proven -> confirmed taxonomy rename (TzolkinB/skills#126, v0.3, ADR-0034) plus per-input sha256 subjects binding the decision to the exact ingested bytes (TzolkinB/skills#139, v0.4, ADR-0037 §2) plus an OPTIONAL top-level `dsseEnvelope` — a self-signed ed25519 DSSE envelope over the bundle's `subject` + the gate entry's `predicate` (TzolkinB/skills#141, v0.5, ADR-0037 §1) — additive only, no shape change to `resourceDescriptor` or existing entries. Backward-compatible with v0.4: a bundle with no `dsseEnvelope` still validates (signing is opt-in), and one with only the `pr-head` subject (no input digests) still validates too, since `subject` only requires ≥1 entry. Execution evidence = Playwright JSON and/or a Cypress Module API result (added additively per contract Q1: `stage` is a free string, so a new framework needs no schema change or version bump); no numeric confidence. audit-test rides either OPAQUE (Markdown inline, caps at canary) or PARSED (verdict.result+label — the B→A graduation, TzolkinB/skills#49; PASSED+confirmed unlocks ship). Extensibility = versioned predicateType URIs + ignore-unrecognized + per-entry annotations.

subject.description: The bundle's content-addressed subjects (contract Q4): always the `pr-head` git-commit subject, plus one sha256-digest subject per ingested input file (TzolkinB/skills#139) — swap an input's bytes and its digest, and so its subject, changes. Digests are lowercase hex strings living here, never in the gate predicate (honesty guard #3 is unaffected). Per-entry subject is reserved for future cross-run aggregation.

dsseEnvelope.description: OPTIONAL (TzolkinB/skills#141, ADR-0037 §1). A DSSE envelope — https://github.com/secure-systems-lab/dsse — self-signing this bundle: `payload` is base64 of `{_type, predicateType, subject, predicate}` reconstructed from THIS bundle's `subject` and its `gate` entry's `predicate`; the signature is over the DSSE pre-authentication encoding, not the raw payload bytes. Absent by default (unsigned bundles are unaffected, honest ceiling stays 'in-toto-shaped, not signed'). Self-signed ed25519 proves integrity + continuity, never third-party identity — not Sigstore.

payload.description: base64 of the serialized signed Statement

keyid.description: sha256 of the ed25519 public key (SPKI/DER)

sig.description: base64 ed25519 signature over the DSSE PAE

producer.description: SLSA builder/metadata — who produced this evidence and when.

statement.description: An in-toto-shaped Statement (ADR-0032) carrying one stage's evidence — kept readable, never itself DSSE/base64-wrapped (that would falsely imply the stage's own producer signed it, ADR-0037 §1). Only the bundle's own gate entry may be covered by the bundle-level `dsseEnvelope`.

evidencePredicate.description: A stage's evidence. `stage` is a data field (contract Q1), so new stages need no schema change.

verdict.comment: verdict.confidence is CUT in v0 (contract Q6). Its arrival is the signal Gate has earned a number — it requires a schemaVersion bump.

gatePredicate.description: The derived gate decision (gate-function spec v0, LOCKED — #103). Honesty guard #3: additionalProperties:false and NO numeric field anywhere — a validator rejects a smuggled number, so re-adding confidence forces a schemaVersion bump.
